title: Postnasal drip
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.
examination.rationale: The exact examination section supports clinician-performed nasal, throat, secretion, crusting, polyp, and postnasal-drip assessment without auto-populating findings.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination

examination.text: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.
examination.rationale: The exact chronic-cough history section was reviewed for adult cough and upper-airway context, but no cough diagnosis or treatment text was mapped.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

investigations.text: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.
investigations.rationale: The exact investigation context supports review of existing specialist information and clinician-entered plan or follow-up without ordering tests or recommending medicine.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 9.1–9.8 — allergy testing and investigation context

medication.text: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.
medication.rationale: The exact rhinitis history section supports onset, nasal discharge, throat clearing or cough context, triggers, obstruction, sleep impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, and concerns without asserting rhinitis, reflux, or an upper-airway cough cause.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors

source_limitations: Duration, nasal discharge, cough, throat clearing, allergy, infection, reflux, smoking, medicines, examination, imaging, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
